title: Preparation Phase
subtitle: Supporting your body for the final weeks
description: As your body prepares for the final stages of pregnancy, you may experience increased pressure and discomfort. Prioritizing rest, maintaining supportive positions, and staying aware of your body's signals can help you stay comfortable and prepared.
what_to_expect: Increased pressure in the lower body and pelvis
Greater discomfort during movement and rest
Heightened awareness of body changes and readiness signals
daily_safety_tips: Prioritize rest and avoid unnecessary physical effort
Use supportive pillows and positions to reduce pressure
Move slowly and carefully during all activities
movement_guidelines: Limit movement to gentle and essential activities
Avoid any movement that causes discomfort or strain
Use support or assistance whenever needed
environmental_awareness: Ensure surroundings are safe, stable, and easily accessible
Keep pathways clear to prevent trips or falls
Continue avoiding exposure to harmful chemicals or smoke
travel_safety: Take breaks to adjust posture if travel cannot be avoided
important_note: Listening to your body and prioritizing rest can help you stay comfortable during this phase.
reassurance: With support and gentle care, you can feel more at ease as you approach the final weeks.
medical_disclaimer: Consult a qualified healthcare professional before starting or modifying any physical activity.

diabetes.personalized_description: Maintaining consistent meals and minimal activity supports stable blood sugar levels during this phase.
movement_guidelines: Engage in minimal gentle movement when comfortable
Maintain consistent meal timing and hydration
Monitor your body's response to reduced activity
diabetes.safety_focus: Watch for fatigue, dizziness, or changes in energy levels.
diabetes.reassurance: Balanced routines support stability and comfort.

anemia.personalized_description: Increased fatigue and discomfort require maximum rest and minimal exertion.
movement_guidelines: Limit activity to essential tasks only
Take frequent and extended rest periods
anemia.safety_focus: Be aware of dizziness, weakness, or breathlessness.
anemia.reassurance: Rest and minimal effort help maintain comfort.

hypertension.personalized_description: Maintaining a calm environment and avoiding strain supports stable blood pressure.
movement_guidelines: Avoid stressful or physically demanding situations
Engage in minimal and controlled movements
hypertension.safety_focus: Be mindful of stress levels and physical strain.
hypertension.reassurance: A calm and steady approach supports well-being.

thyroid.personalized_description: Consistent routines and minimal activity support metabolic balance during this stage.
movement_guidelines: Maintain a steady and minimal activity pattern
thyroid.safety_focus: Be aware of unusual fatigue or energy fluctuations.

multi_condition_blend.combined_message: When managing multiple conditions, prioritizing rest, support, and minimal movement becomes critical during this phase.
priority_guidelines: Avoid unnecessary physical effort or strain
Maintain minimal and essential movement only
Take frequent rest breaks and stay hydrated
multi_condition_blend.reassurance: Careful rest and support help maintain comfort and safety.
